arabic_text:
دور کشف خالص
دور فترت
دور ستر
قائم دور کشف
قائم دور ستر
خالص کشفیہ صورات
خالص کشفیۃ صورات
فتریۃ صورات
english_text:
Pure Cycle of Revelation — 42,000 years (pure revelatory forms)
Pure Cycle of Revelation — 4000 years (pure revelatory forms)
Cycle of Interlude (Fatrat) — 3000 years (pure revelatory forms)
Cycle of Interlude — 3000 years (interlude forms)
Cycle of Concealment (Sitr) — 7000 years
Qa'im of the Cycle of Revelation (50,000)
Qa'im of the Cycle of Concealment (10,000)
alt_text: Two adjacent yellow tabular columns decomposing larger cycles into sub-periods. Right column (overlapping): 'دور کشف خالص 42,000 برس (خالص کشفیۃ صورات)' (Pure Revelation 42,000 yrs, pure revelatory forms), 'دور کشف خالص 4000 برس' (Pure Revelation 4000 yrs), 'دور فترت 3000 برس (خالص کشفیہ صورات)' (Interlude 3000 yrs, pure revelatory forms). Highlighted red box at bottom: 'قائم دور کشف (50,000)' (Qa'im of Revelation Cycle, 50,000). Left column: 'دور فترت 3000 برس (فتریہ صورات)' (Interlude 3000 yrs, interlude forms), 'دور ستر 7000 برس' (Concealment 7000 yrs). Highlighted black box at bottom: 'قائم دور ستر (10,000)' (Qa'im of Concealment Cycle, 10,000).
notes: Decomposition of the Daur into sub-periods: 42K + 4K + 3K (Revelation pure forms) + 3K (Interlude forms) + 7K (Concealment) sub-cycles, capped by the Qa'im positions of 50K (Revelation) and 10K (Concealment). The 'خالص' (pure) vs 'فتریہ' (interlude-type) form distinction is the key new concept.
